recommended.title: ACP Decision Lifecycle Workflow
recommended.why: ACP now has fixture records, default registry inclusion, recovery/export surfaces, hardened RDF projection diagnostics, and a non-writing diff mode. The next control-plane gap is safe lifecycle governance for decisions.
expected_outputs: safe promotion verifier or workflow check
must_preserve: R035, R037, and R038 remain active unless separately proven
decision candidates are not accepted decisions until workflow checks pass
proof gate records are not proof gate satisfaction

alternatives.title: RDF engine spike
alternatives.when_to_choose: Choose only if engine-executed RDF/SPARQL proof becomes valuable for ACP interoperability or recovery work.
alternatives.boundary: A spike must not promote RDF output to architecture source truth.

alternatives.title: SHACL engine spike
alternatives.when_to_choose: Choose only if actual SHACL engine validation becomes valuable beyond deterministic structural smoke.
alternatives.boundary: A spike must not claim ontology correctness without separate proof.

alternatives.title: Isolated git-lex alignment spike
alternatives.when_to_choose: Choose only if identity/provenance alignment with git-lex becomes the priority.
alternatives.boundary: Do not initialize git-lex in the main repository during the spike.

alternatives.title: ACP recovery/dashboard views
alternatives.when_to_choose: Choose if human/operator navigation becomes more urgent than lifecycle governance.
alternatives.boundary: Dashboard or recovery views remain derived diagnostics.

planning_constraints: Use GSD milestone/slice/task tools for planning and closeout.
Run GitNexus impact before editing code symbols.
Run GitNexus detect changes before commits.
Use tracked relative source anchors only.
Avoid ignored execution-log anchors and absolute local paths.
Keep product/legal/runtime/parser/FalkorDB/retrieval claims bounded unless separate proof exists.
